w:r.w:t: Crypto traders, DeFi investors, market researchers

Bloomberg Terminal costs $24,000 per year. It is the single most used tool in professional finance. The reason is not its data — it is its interconnectedness. Click any entity anywhere and everything else on screen reorganizes around it. Traders pay $2,000/month just for that traversal.

No equivalent exists for crypto-native users. Existing crypto dashboards are siloed: one tab for news, another for prices, another for ETF flows, another for macro calendars. Every time you want to connect two pieces of information you open a new tab.

CryptoDesk is the first crypto terminal where everything is connected. Built entirely on SoSoValue's API — the only data provider that combines ETF flows, macro events, SSI indices, BTC treasuries, crypto stocks, and news in a single stack — CryptoDesk lets any user start anywhere and reach any related data in two clicks, then act on it directly via SoDEX.
CryptoDesk is what you get when you give Bloomberg's interconnectedness model to a crypto-native data stack — built on SoSoValue, executed on SoDEX.
2.1 What is the current user experience?
Meet a typical crypto investor. They want to understand one thing: 'CPI drops tomorrow — should I adjust my BTC position?'
Open a macro calendar site to find the CPI date and forecast number.
Open CoinDesk or SoSoValue to find ETF flow data — read 2–3 articles to get a number.
Open TradingView for BTC price chart.
Open Twitter to read analysts — gets 15 contradictory opinions.
Try to remember what happened last time CPI dropped.
Give up. Hold. Gets surprised by the move.
The information to make this decision exists. It is scattered across 5+ tools. The user does not have time to synthesize it. So they act on incomplete information — or do not act at all.
Three things that Bloomberg has and crypto tools do not:
Connected data — click a news headline and see the related price chart and ETF flow automatically.
Context traversal — start anywhere (news, price, event) and reach any related data in two clicks.
Integrated execution — see the signal and act on it in the same screen, without switching tools.

A single-page terminal with a universal search bar and three persistent panels. Search any entity — a crypto asset, a macro event, a stock, an index — and all three panels instantly reorganize to show connected data about that entity.
News, events, and alerts related to the searched entity. Real-time, filterable by category.
Price chart or performance chart for the entity, with macro event overlays, ETF flow bars, and index constituent data layered on top.
Live SoDEX price, scenario brief (AI-generated), trade setup suggestion, and execution button. SoDEX is load-bearing here.

Every search term resolves into one of four contexts. Each context pulls different SoSoValue endpoints but renders in the same three-panel layout. This is the Bloomberg insight: one interface, infinite contexts.
Search: BTC, ETH, SOL, or any supported currency
Live news filtered to this asset. Category tabs: News / Research / KOL / Announcements.
Daily price chart. ETF net inflow bars overlaid. Macro event dates marked as vertical lines. Click any event line to expand it in Panel A.
Live SoDEX spot price. 24h change. ATH distance. AI-generated 2-sentence market brief. 'Trade on SoDEX' button.
Search: CPI, FOMC, NFP, Nonfarm Payrolls
News filtered to this event keyword. Countdown timer to next release. Previous actual vs forecast.
Surprise delta chart: every historical release plotted as green (beat) or red (miss) bar. BTC 24h reaction overlaid as a line. Pattern is visible immediately.
AI scenario brief: 'Last 8 times CPI beat forecast by >0.1%, BTC averaged +3.1% in 24h.' Live SoDEX BTC price. Bull/Bear scenario cards with suggested levels.
Search: MSTR, COIN, MARA, RIOT, CLSK
Crypto stock news (category 13). Filtered to this ticker. Company intro and social links from /crypto-stocks.
Stock price chart (daily). BTC treasury holdings bar chart overlaid. Each BTC purchase event marked. P/E and P/B ratios in sidebar.
Sector performance vs BTC. AI brief: 'MSTR holds 214,400 BTC at avg cost $35,000. Current BTC price implies X% unrealized gain on treasury.' Correlation stat: MSTR vs BTC 30-day.
Search: MAG7, Layer1, DeFi, or any SSI index ticker
Index constituent list with weights. News aggregated across all constituents. Performance table: which constituent is up/down today.
Index price chart. 1m, 3m, 1y ROI. Constituent weight pie chart. Individual constituent performance bars.
Rebalancing calculator: 'Enter portfolio size → see exact trades needed to match this index today.' Trade list with SoDEX prices. 'Execute on SoDEX' button (Wave 2).

CryptoDesk has two layers: a thin Node.js backend that proxies and caches SoSoValue API calls, and a React frontend that renders the three-panel terminal UI. The backend exists for one reason: the SoSoValue rate limit is 20 requests/minute. Without a caching proxy, the live demo breaks within 2 minutes.
Cache strategy: in-memory Map keyed by route + params. TTL checked on every request. On cache miss, upstream call fires, response stored. No Redis needed for Wave 1 demo.
State management: Zustand (lightweight, no Redux boilerplate)
Charts: Recharts — ComposedChart for overlay data (price + ETF bars + event markers)
Styling: Tailwind CSS — dark theme, monospace font for terminal aesthetic
Search: debounced input (300ms), resolves to one of 4 context types via keyword mapping
WebSocket: SoDEX live price stream for Panel C (wss://mainnet-gw.sodex.dev/ws/spot)
The search bar is the heart of CryptoDesk. When a user types, the frontend resolves the input to a context type:
if input matches known SSI ticker (ssimag7, ssilayer1...) → Context 4 (Index)
if input matches crypto stock ticker (MSTR, COIN, MARA...) → Context 3 (Stock)
if input matches macro keyword (CPI, FOMC, NFP...) → Context 2 (Macro)
Primary data source — news, ETF, macro, indices, currencies, BTC treasuries, crypto stocks. All proxied through backend.
Live price ticker (public, no auth). Order placement in Wave 2 only.
1-hour klines for macro reaction analysis (Context 2 only). Free, no auth required.
AI narrative generation via Mistral-7B-Instruct or DeepSeek-chat. Free tier. Used only for Panel C briefs.

Wave 1 delivers four search contexts, three panels, real API data, and a working demo. No wallet connect. No user accounts. No mobile optimization. No alerts. Four contexts done well beats eight done badly.
Repo init, Vite + Tailwind scaffold, Railway backend deployed, SoSoValue API key applied for
API approval delay — mitigate by mocking SoSoValue responses with static JSON
All 8 backend routes working with in-memory cache, tested with curl, rate limit safe
Cache key collisions — test each route independently
React layout: search bar + three panels, responsive, dark theme, no data yet
Layout complexity — use CSS Grid, not Flexbox nesting
BTC search works end-to-end: news in Panel A, price chart in Panel B, SoDEX ticker in Panel C
SoDEX WebSocket on localhost — test CORS headers early
ETF net inflow bars overlaid on BTC price chart. Macro event vertical lines on chart.
Recharts ComposedChart alignment — test with dummy data first
CPI search works: surprise delta chart in Panel B, scenario cards in Panel C, news in Panel A
Binance kline alignment with macro dates — date parsing bugs
AI-generated narrative in Panel C for both Currency and Macro contexts. Cached per prompt.
OpenRouter rate limits on free tier — test early, add fallback static text
MSTR search: stock chart + BTC treasury overlaid in Panel B, crypto stock news in Panel A
BTC treasury purchase history sparse for some tickers — handle empty states
MAG7 search: index chart + constituents in Panel A, rebalancing calculator in Panel C
Index klines only daily — calculator must use live /market-snapshot prices
Panel transitions feel instant. Loading skeletons on all panels. Empty states handled gracefully.
Skeleton flash on fast loads — add minimum loading time of 300ms
90-second screen recording. Four context walkthroughs. GitHub README complete. Vercel deploy live.
Video quality — use Loom or OBS, record 3 takes, use best one
Submit on AKINDO platform before deadline. All required fields complete.
Deadline timezone — confirm May 12 EOD is UTC or IST

Judges evaluate all three waves as a connected arc. Wave 1 wins on vision clarity. Wave 2 wins on execution. Wave 3 wins on completeness. Each wave must answer: 'What did you add that makes this more valuable?'
Four search contexts fully working with real SoSoValue API data
Three-panel terminal UI with dark theme and Bloomberg-inspired traversal
SoDEX live price in Panel C (no auth, WebSocket)
Binance 1h klines for macro reaction analysis in Context 2
Live demo on Vercel + public GitHub repo
SoDEX wallet connect via MetaMask — EIP-712 signing implemented
One-click order placement from Panel C (limit orders, spot)
SSI Index rebalancing executor — computes and submits batch orders on SoDEX
Watchlist — user pins entities, persisted in localStorage
Alert system — browser notifications when macro event is within 24 hours
ETH, SOL, XRP added as supported assets (Context 1 expansion)
GET /analyses endpoint integrated — discover and render dynamic chart types
Automated rebalancing agent — scheduled SSI index rebalancing via SoDEX cron
Portfolio tracker — connect wallet, see holdings vs target index allocation
Custom index builder — user defines own basket weights, tracks vs SSI benchmark
Multi-asset macro correlation view — how does FOMC affect ETH vs BTC vs crypto stocks?
Perps integration — SoDEX perps API for hedge positions on macro events
Export functionality — download analysis as PDF report
SoSoValue judges score on five dimensions. Here is how CryptoDesk maps to each, and what specifically in the demo addresses each criterion.
Directly solves the 'five tabs open' problem. A trader gets macro context, ETF signal, price data, and trade execution in one screen.
Show the CPI search: news + surprise chart + AI brief + SoDEX price, all in one view. Ask: 'How many tabs would this replace?'
Four live search contexts, real API data, real SoDEX WebSocket price. No mocked responses in demo.
Type BTC → panels load with live data. Type CPI → surprise chart renders with historical pattern. Type MSTR → treasury overlay appears.
Bloomberg traversal model is a recognized, proven architecture. Three-panel layout is industry standard (Refinitiv, FactSet). Not invented — adapted.
Show that clicking a macro event line on the BTC chart jumps Panel A to news about that event. The connection is live.
Uses 7 of SoSoValue's 8 API modules. SoDEX public endpoints. Binance. OpenRouter. No module is just a list — each drives a visual.
In submission doc: list every endpoint called, the module it belongs to, and which panel it feeds. Full coverage story.
Dark terminal aesthetic signals professional intent. Search bar as single entry point = zero learning curve. Panel labels are plain English.
First 5 seconds of video: someone who has never used CryptoDesk types 'BTC' and immediately understands what they are looking at.
GET https://mainnet-gw.sodex.dev/api/v1/spot/markets/tickers — live spot prices, no auth
wss://mainnet-gw.sodex.dev/ws/spot — streaming price updates for Panel C
GET https://api.binance.com/api/v3/klines?symbol=BTCUSDT&interval=1h — intraday reaction data for Context 2 macro charts
POST https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions — Mistral-7B or DeepSeek-chat for scenario briefs in Panel C
Scope discipline is the difference between a working demo and a broken ambitious thing. The following are explicitly deferred to Wave 2 or Wave 3. Do not touch these in Wave 1.
Actual SoDEX order placement — EIP-712 signing (Wave 2)
User accounts, login, or saved preferences (Wave 2)
WebSocket reconnect logic — use polling fallback if WS drops (acceptable for demo)
More than 4 search contexts — BTC, ETH, SOL are enough for Wave 1 asset coverage
Project overview submitted on AKINDO: name, description, target users, API list, core logic
Public GitHub repo at github.com/anxbt/cryptodesk with complete README
README includes: setup instructions, env vars list, npm install + npm run dev steps
Live demo deployed on Vercel — URL accessible without login
90-second video on Loom or YouTube — four context walkthrough
Team information: solo builder, contact info
Wave 1 changelog: 'Implemented four search contexts, three-panel layout, SoSoValue full API integration, SoDEX live price, OpenRouter AI briefs'
Hook: 'Bloomberg Terminal costs $24,000/year. It is powerful because everything is connected. CryptoDesk does the same thing for crypto — free, on-chain.'
Demo: Type BTC. Show panels loading with live data. Point at ETF overlay on chart. Point at SoDEX live price in Panel C.
Demo: Type CPI. Show surprise delta chart. Show AI scenario brief. 'This is what happened last 8 times CPI beat forecast.'
Demo: Type MSTR. Show stock + BTC treasury overlay. Demo: Type MAG7. Show index constituents + rebalancing calculator.
Roadmap: 'Wave 2: execute the trade from Panel C via SoDEX. Wave 3: automated agent rebalances your index position every week.'
Close: 'CryptoDesk. Built on SoSoValue. Executed on SoDEX. The terminal the ecosystem deserved.'
SoSoValue API key approval takes >3 days
Build with mocked JSON responses. Swap to live calls when key arrives. Do not block frontend work.
Implement REST polling fallback at 5s interval. WebSocket is enhancement, not dependency.
OpenRouter free tier rate limited during demo
Cache AI briefs aggressively (10 min TTL). Pre-generate briefs for the 4 demo searches. Have static fallback text.
Recharts ComposedChart alignment breaks with mixed data
Test overlay chart in isolation on Day 3 with dummy data. Do not discover alignment bugs on Day 9.
SoSoValue 20 req/min limit hit during judge review
Backend cache ensures max 1 upstream call per endpoint per TTL window. Judge never sees a 429.
Binance klines timezone mismatch with macro dates
Normalize all timestamps to UTC at data ingestion layer in backend.
Based on the hackathon brief, judge profile (SoSoValue team), and typical AKINDO submission patterns, here is what other teams will build and why CryptoDesk is different.
Smart Research Dashboard — a page with charts from /news and /etfs endpoints
Uses 2–3 SoSoValue modules. No SoDEX integration. No traversal. Just a dashboard.
Uses 7 of 8 modules. SoDEX is load-bearing. Traversal is the product.
Telegram bot that summarizes news with GPT-4
No SoDEX. No visual. Judges cannot see a demo. Telegram bots feel like demos, not products.
Web app with live panels. Video shows something visual and immediate.
Generic architecture. Signal quality is fabricated. Judges cannot verify the signal logic.
Signal is the historical pattern from real SoSoValue macro + ETF data. Math is visible in the chart.
No SoSoValue unique data advantage. CoinGecko does this better.
Uses SSI indices — SoSoValue's own product — as the copy target. Judges see their product used.
Single module. No connection to other data. SoSoValue already shows this on their own site.
ETF flow is one overlay in a larger connected terminal. Not the product — part of the product.
CryptoDesk — SoSoValue Buildathon 2026 | Built by Rishav (anxbt) | Wave 1 PRD v1.0
